input_row.title: Ensure 'FTP State' is set to 'FTPS only' or 'Disabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to the Azure Portal 2. Select App Services 3. Click on an app 4. Select Settings and then Configuration 5. Under General Settings, for the Platform Settings, the FTP state should be set to Disabled or FTPS Only Remediate from Azure CLI For each out of compliance application, run the following choosing either 'disabled' or 'FtpsOnly' as appropriate: az webapp config set --resource-group <resource group name> --name <app name> --ftps-state [disabled|FtpsOnly] Remediate from PowerShell For each out of compliance application, run the following: Set-AzWebApp -ResourceGroupName <resource group name> -Name <app name> - FtpsState <Disabled or FtpsOnly> Default Value: By default, FTP state is set to FTPS only. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/appservice/web-apps/create-or-update- configuration#ftpsstate 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp 8. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/set-azwebapp CIS Controls:
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/deploy-ftp 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-security 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- data-protection#dp-4-encrypt-sensitive-information-in-transit 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/appservice/web-apps/create-or-update- configuration#ftpsstate 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/get-azwebapp 8. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.websites/set-azwebapp CIS Controls: Controls Version Control IG 1 IG 2 IG 3 v8 3.10 Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH). ● ● v7 14.4 Encrypt All Sensitive Information in Transit Encrypt all sensitive information in transit.  ● ● v7 16.5 Encrypt Transmittal of Username and Authentication Credentials Ensure that all account usernames and authentication credentials are transmitted across networks using encrypted channels. ● ●

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field is empty, but based on the remediation and references provided, we can deduce that the control can be verified via Azure CLI and PowerShell commands.
- The control specifies configurations related to App Services, which can be checked directly using Azure APIs or CLI commands for resource properties.
- The existence of specific CLI commands implies the property is queryable, confirming it as an automated control.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_ftp_state_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The program would check the FTPS state of Azure App Services using the Azure CLI `az webapp config show` command.
- Resources to enumerate will include all App Services across specified subscriptions or resource groups.
- The specific property to validate is `ftpsState`.
- The clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: `ftpsState` is either "Disabled" or "FtpsOnly".
  - Fail: `ftpsState` is set to any value other than "Disabled" or "FtpsOnly".
- Example CLI command for verification:
  ```bash
  az webapp config show --resource-group <resource_group_name> --name <app_name> --query ftpsState
  ```

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is
- The assessment is accurate and aligns with Azure security best practices. The automated approach is justified, and the technical execution is correct. The minor improvements suggested do not necessitate a change in the classification or the core approach.
